title: HTTP Fundamentals
description: The stateless request/response model, methods and idempotency, headers, and the evolution from HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 and HTTP/3.

title: HTTP Status Codes
description: The five classes of status codes and the ones that matter: 200/201/204, 301 vs 302, 304, 400/401/403/404/409/422/429, and 5xx.

title: HTTPS & TLS
description: How TLS secures the web: the handshake that uses asymmetric keys to agree a symmetric session key, certificates, and stopping man-in-the-middle.

title: How Cookies Work
description: How Set-Cookie and the Cookie header carry small key/values per request, and the attributes that matter: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite, expiry.

title: JWT & Stateless Auth
description: The header.payload.signature structure, why a JWT is signed (not encrypted), stateless validation with no lookup, and the revocation trade-off.

title: CORS
description: The same-origin policy, why the browser (not the server) blocks cross-origin reads, the preflight OPTIONS request, and configuring CORS in ASP.NET Core.

title: REST API Design
description: REST as a style: resources as nouns identified by URIs, HTTP methods as actions, statelessness, status codes, and the Richardson maturity model.

title: How DNS Works
description: How a domain resolves to an IP through resolver, root, TLD and authoritative servers, the common record types, and the role of TTL and caching.

title: OAuth 2.0 & OpenID Connect
description: Delegated authorization without sharing your password: the Authorization Code flow with PKCE, scopes, tokens, and how OpenID Connect adds identity.

title: WebSockets vs Polling vs SSE
description: Four ways to push real-time data to the browser — short polling, long polling, Server-Sent Events, and WebSockets — and when to choose each.

title: HTTP Caching
description: How HTTP caching avoids re-fetching: Cache-Control freshness, ETag/Last-Modified validation and 304, no-cache vs no-store, and cache busting.

title: Content Negotiation
description: How client and server agree on the best response representation using Accept headers — media types, compression, and the Vary header.

title: How Sessions Work
description: How a server remembers who you are across stateless HTTP requests using an opaque session ID in a cookie.

title: Load Balancers & Reverse Proxies
description: How load balancers spread traffic for scale and HA — Layer 4 vs Layer 7, balancing algorithms, health checks, sticky sessions, and the reverse proxy role.

title: CDNs
description: How a CDN caches content on edge servers close to users to cut latency and offload the origin — edge caching, push vs pull, invalidation, and routing.

title: Caching at Scale
description: The layers of caching in a real system — browser, CDN, application, distributed (Redis/Memcached), database — plus hit ratio and Redis vs Memcached.

title: Message Queues (RabbitMQ & Kafka)
description: How message queues decouple producers from consumers, smooth spikes and add resilience, plus queue (RabbitMQ) vs log/stream (Kafka) and delivery guarantees.

title: Rate Limiting Algorithms
description: How to cap request rates fairly: fixed window, sliding window, token bucket and leaky bucket, returning 429 with Retry-After, and distributed limits with Redis.

title: Consistent Hashing
description: Why hash(key) % N falls apart when N changes, and how consistent hashing maps keys and nodes onto a ring so resizing moves only a small fraction of keys.

title: CAP Theorem
description: Why a distributed system must choose between Consistency and Availability during a network partition — CP vs AP with examples, plus PACELC.

title: Horizontal vs Vertical Scaling
description: Scale up (a bigger machine) vs scale out (more machines): the ceiling and single point of failure of vertical, why horizontal needs stateless services.

title: How Twitter's Timeline Works
description: The fan-out problem behind a home timeline: fan-out-on-write (push) vs fan-out-on-read (pull), the celebrity problem, and the hybrid approach.

title: How Instagram's Feed Works
description: Beyond fan-out: a ranked feed from candidate generation, ML ranking and CDN-delivered media, plus sharded time-sortable ID generation and caching.

title: How Netflix Streaming Works
description: Video at scale: adaptive bitrate streaming with chunked multi-bitrate encodes, a CDN at the edge, a transcoding pipeline, and a microservices control plane.

title: How Gmail Works
description: Webmail at scale: sharded storage, an inverted index for instant search, real-time push delivery, the label model, and ML spam filtering.

title: Async / Await Internals
description: What async/await really does in .NET: the compiler state machine, freeing the thread on I/O, and avoiding deadlocks.

title: The CLR (Common Language Runtime)
description: .NET's virtual machine: loads assemblies, JIT-compiles IL to native, runs the GC, and enforces type safety.

title: JIT Compilation
description: How the .NET JIT turns IL into native code on first call, plus tiered compilation, ReadyToRun and how JIT differs from AOT.

title: Garbage Collection
description: How the .NET GC reclaims memory: the generational heap (gen0/1/2), mark-sweep-compact, the LOH, and IDisposable.

title: Memory Management
description: Stack vs heap, value vs reference types, boxing, and cutting allocations with structs, Span<T>, stackalloc and ArrayPool.

title: Assemblies & Metadata
description: What a .NET assembly is — IL, metadata and a manifest in a .dll — the unit of deployment, plus reflection and AssemblyLoadContext.

title: Task vs Thread
description: Why a Task isn't a Thread: Thread is a heavy OS resource you manage; Task is a lightweight future that often uses no thread for I/O.

title: The Thread Pool
description: How .NET reuses worker threads for short work items, how the pool grows under load, and why blocking causes thread-pool starvation.

title: IHostedService & BackgroundService
description: Running background work tied to the app lifetime with BackgroundService: the ExecuteAsync loop, graceful shutdown, and scoped services.

title: The Middleware Pipeline
description: How ASP.NET Core runs every request through an ordered middleware pipeline — the onion model, short-circuiting, and why order matters.

title: Dependency Injection
description: How the .NET DI container resolves dependency graphs, the singleton/scoped/transient lifetimes, and the captive-dependency trap.

title: The Configuration System
description: How .NET layers configuration from JSON, environment files, env vars and secrets into one IConfiguration, with the typed Options pattern.

title: Logging (Serilog / NLog)
description: Structured logging in .NET: the ILogger abstraction, providers and sinks, log levels, and why message templates beat string interpolation.

title: Health Checks
description: How ASP.NET Core health checks tell orchestrators if the app is alive and ready — liveness vs readiness, dependency checks, the /health endpoint.

title: HttpClientFactory
description: Why newing HttpClient per request exhausts sockets and a forever-singleton goes stale on DNS, and how IHttpClientFactory pools handlers and adds Polly resilience.

title: Authentication & Authorization
description: The ASP.NET Core auth pipeline: authentication builds a ClaimsPrincipal; authorization enforces policies. Schemes, claims, and 401 vs 403.

title: Minimal APIs
description: ASP.NET Core Minimal APIs: endpoints as lambdas with no controllers, parameter binding from route/query/body/DI, IResult, route groups and filters.

title: EF Core Internals
description: How EF Core works: the DbContext as a unit of work, the change tracker, LINQ-to-SQL translation, the N+1 problem, and AsNoTracking.

title: SignalR
description: Real-time server-to-client push in .NET: hubs as RPC, transport negotiation (WebSockets first), groups, and the Redis backplane for scale-out.

title: gRPC
description: Schema-first RPC over HTTP/2 with Protocol Buffers — strongly-typed contracts, four streaming modes, and when to choose it over REST/JSON.

title: Blazor Basics
description: Build interactive web UI in C# with components. Blazor Server (UI over SignalR) vs WebAssembly (C# in the browser), and .NET 8 render modes.

title: Span<T> & Memory<T>
description: Span<T> is a zero-copy, bounds-checked window over contiguous memory. Why it's allocation-free, the ref-struct rules, and when to use Memory<T>.

title: Records
description: C# records: compiler-generated value equality, concise syntax, init-only immutability, and non-destructive 'with' copies for DTOs and value objects.

title: Nullable Reference Types
description: How C# nullable reference types track null at compile time — string vs string?, flow analysis, the null-forgiving operator, and why it's not runtime enforcement.

title: Global Usings
description: How C# 10 global using directives and the SDK's ImplicitUsings remove repeated imports from every file — plus global using static and aliases.

title: Pattern Matching
description: C# pattern matching: switch expressions, type/property/relational/logical and list patterns — replacing verbose if/else and casts.

title: Source Generators
description: Roslyn source generators run at compile time and emit extra C# — replacing runtime reflection for JSON, regex and logging, and enabling AOT.

title: Native AOT
description: Native AOT compiles a .NET app to a self-contained native binary ahead of time — no JIT, instant startup, low memory — and its trade-offs.

title: Database Indexing
description: How an index turns a full table scan (O(n)) into a B-tree seek (O(log n)) — clustered, non-clustered and covering indexes.

title: Reading Execution Plans
description: How the optimizer turns SQL into a plan using statistics, how to read it (seek vs scan, joins, key lookups), and why stale stats and parameter sniffing hurt.

title: Transactions & ACID
description: What a transaction guarantees — Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability — and how commit/rollback and the write-ahead log survive a crash.

title: Isolation Levels
description: The four SQL isolation levels and the read phenomena they prevent (dirty, non-repeatable, phantom), plus snapshot/MVCC and the consistency-vs-concurrency trade-off.

title: Deadlocks
description: Why two transactions can lock each other forever: the circular wait, how the DB kills a victim, and prevention via lock ordering, short transactions and retries.

title: Normalization vs Denormalization
description: Normalization removes redundancy for write integrity (1NF-3NF); denormalization duplicates data for read speed. The trade-off and when to use each.

title: Connection Pooling
description: Why opening a DB connection is expensive, how a pool reuses open connections, what causes pool exhaustion, and why disposing matters.

title: Partitioning & Sharding
description: Scaling by splitting data: vertical vs horizontal partitioning, sharding across nodes by a shard key, avoiding hotspots, and the cost of cross-shard queries.

title: Replication
description: Leader-follower vs multi-leader, synchronous vs asynchronous, replication lag and eventual consistency, read replicas for scaling reads, and failover.

title: Caching Strategies
description: The main caching patterns — cache-aside, read-through, write-through, write-back, write-around — how they trade freshness for speed, and invalidation.

title: NoSQL Patterns
description: The four NoSQL families — document, key-value, wide-column, graph — what each is good at, denormalization and horizontal scale, and ACID vs BASE.

title: Redis Internals
description: Why Redis is fast: an in-memory, single-threaded data-structure store. Strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams, persistence, and common uses.

title: SQL vs NoSQL
description: When to choose relational vs NoSQL: structure, relationships and ACID vs flexible schema, horizontal scale and BASE — and why the answer is usually both.

title: Stored Procedures vs ORMs
description: The trade-offs between stored procedures (precompiled SQL in the DB) and ORMs like EF Core, plus the pragmatic middle ground of a micro-ORM for hot paths.

title: SOLID Principles
description: The five SOLID principles explained in plain English with C# examples that keep code easy to change.

title: Encapsulation
description: Bundling data with the methods that guard it and hiding state behind a controlled surface to protect invariants.

title: Abstraction
description: Exposing the essential 'what' and hiding the complex 'how' behind a contract so callers depend on a simple surface.

title: Inheritance
description: Modeling an is-a relationship where a derived class reuses and specializes a base — virtual/override, sealed, and favoring composition.

title: Polymorphism
description: One interface, many behaviors: runtime dispatch via virtual/override versus compile-time overloading and generics.

title: Composition vs Inheritance
description: Why favor composition over inheritance: has-a beats is-a for flexibility, avoids fragile base classes, and swaps behavior at runtime.

title: Abstract Class vs Interface
description: When to use an abstract class vs an interface in C#: shared implementation and state vs a pure capability contract, and single vs multiple inheritance.

title: Coupling & Cohesion
description: Keep coupling (dependency between modules) low and cohesion (focus within a module) high — the two forces behind maintainable design.

title: Law of Demeter
description: The principle of least knowledge: only talk to immediate friends and avoid train-wreck chains like a.GetB().GetC().Do() to cut coupling.

title: DRY, KISS & YAGNI
description: Three pragmatic heuristics: one source of truth (DRY), keep it simple (KISS), don't build for imagined futures (YAGNI) — and their tension.

title: Monolith vs Microservices
description: When to choose a monolith vs microservices: simplicity vs independent deploy/scale, the distributed-systems tax, bounded contexts, and starting monolith.

title: API Gateway
description: Why microservices put a single API gateway in front of clients — routing, auth, rate limiting, TLS, aggregation, protocol translation — plus BFF and the SPOF risk.

title: Service Discovery
description: How services find each other when instances come and go: the registry, client-side vs server-side discovery, health-based registration, and Kubernetes DNS.

title: Inter-Service Communication
description: How microservices talk: synchronous REST/gRPC (simple but coupled, cascading failures) vs asynchronous events (decoupled, resilient, eventually consistent).

title: Event-Driven Architecture
description: Services collaborate by producing/consuming events: loose coupling via pub/sub, choreography vs orchestration, eventual consistency, and event sourcing.

title: Saga Pattern
description: Keeping data consistent across services without distributed ACID: a sequence of local transactions with compensating actions to undo on failure.

title: Outbox Pattern
description: The dual-write problem and the transactional outbox: write the event to an outbox table in the same DB transaction, then a relay publishes it (at-least-once).

title: Circuit Breaker
description: Stop hammering a failing dependency by failing fast: the closed/open/half-open state machine, fallbacks, preventing cascading failures, and Polly in .NET.

title: Retry, Timeout & Bulkhead
description: Resilience patterns beyond the circuit breaker: retry with backoff and jitter (idempotent only), timeouts on every call, and bulkhead resource isolation.

title: Distributed Tracing
description: Follow one request across many services: a trace made of spans, context propagation via the trace ID, and OpenTelemetry/Jaeger to pinpoint the slow service.

title: Centralized Logging
description: Why per-instance logs are useless and how centralized logging aggregates structured logs into one searchable store (ELK/Loki/Seq), correlated by trace ID.

title: Docker & Containers
description: Containers package an app plus its dependencies into a portable isolated unit. Images vs containers, layers, the Dockerfile, and containers vs VMs.

title: Kubernetes Concepts
description: Container orchestration: Pods, Deployments, Services and Ingress; declarative desired state; self-healing, autoscaling, rolling updates; control plane and nodes.

title: Microservices with .NET
description: The .NET ecosystem for microservices mapped to the patterns: YARP/Ocelot gateways, MassTransit messaging/sagas/outbox, Dapr sidecar, Aspire, gRPC, Polly, OpenTelemetry.

title: Singleton Pattern
description: Guaranteeing one shared instance in C# with a thread-safe Lazy<T> — and why DI's singleton lifetime is usually better.

title: Factory Pattern
description: Create objects without hard-coding their concrete type — Simple Factory and Factory Method, decoupling creation from use and honoring Open/Closed.

title: Abstract Factory
description: Create whole families of related objects through one interface so products from the same family are always used together.

title: Builder Pattern
description: Construct a complex object step by step with a fluent API, avoiding telescoping constructors — and where C# initializers/records make it unnecessary.

title: Prototype Pattern
description: Create new objects by cloning an existing one. Shallow vs deep copy, why ICloneable is discouraged, and records' with-expression in C#.

title: Adapter Pattern
description: Wrap an incompatible interface so it fits the one your code expects — integrating third-party/legacy code. Object vs class adapter; vs facade/decorator.

title: Decorator Pattern
description: Add behavior to an object by wrapping it in another with the same interface — stackable, composition-over-inheritance extension, as in .NET Streams.

title: Facade Pattern
description: A single simplified entry point that hides a complex subsystem of many classes — reducing coupling, without changing or adding behavior.

title: Proxy Pattern
description: A stand-in with the same interface that controls access to a real object — virtual (lazy), protection, remote and caching proxies, as in EF Core and gRPC.

title: Composite Pattern
description: Treat individual objects and groups uniformly through one interface, modeling part-whole tree structures like file systems and UI trees.

title: Strategy Pattern
description: Define a family of interchangeable algorithms behind one interface and swap them at runtime — replacing conditionals; in C# a delegate is often the strategy.

title: Observer Pattern
description: A one-to-many dependency where a subject notifies all observers when it changes — and how C# events, delegates and IObservable<T> are observer built in.

title: Command Pattern
description: Wrap a request as an object so you can queue, log and undo it, decoupling invoker from receiver — the basis of undo/redo, task queues, MediatR and CQRS.

title: Template Method Pattern
description: Define the skeleton of an algorithm in a base class and let subclasses fill in steps via overridable hooks — the Hollywood Principle, as in BackgroundService.

title: State Pattern
description: Let an object change behavior when its state changes by delegating to state objects that handle transitions — a clean OO state machine, contrasted with Strategy.

title: Chain of Responsibility
description: Pass a request along a chain of handlers, each handling it or forwarding it — decoupling sender from receiver, exactly like the ASP.NET Core middleware pipeline.

title: Mediator Pattern
description: Route interactions between objects through a central hub instead of direct references, cutting many-to-many coupling — and how MediatR applies it in .NET.

title: CQRS
description: Command Query Responsibility Segregation: separate the write model (commands) from the read model (queries) for optimized reads and scaling — and when not to use it.

title: Repository Pattern
description: A collection-like abstraction over data access that hides the DB/ORM and aids testability — plus the caveat that EF Core's DbContext is already a repository.

title: Unit of Work
description: Track all changes in a business transaction and commit them atomically — and how EF Core's DbContext + SaveChanges already implement it.

title: Specification Pattern
description: Encapsulate a business rule or query as a reusable, composable object combined with And/Or/Not — eliminating duplicated Where clauses in C#.

title: Delegates & Events
description: Delegates are type-safe function pointers (Func/Action, multicast, lambdas); events are a restricted delegate for pub/sub — the basis of callbacks, LINQ and observer.

title: LINQ Internals
description: How LINQ works: deferred execution, IEnumerable (delegates, in-memory) vs IQueryable (expression trees to SQL), and the multiple-enumeration trap.

title: Generics
description: Type-safe, reusable code parameterized by type — no boxing, no casting. Generic classes and methods, constraints, and CLR specialization of value vs reference types.

title: Covariance & Contravariance
description: How generic type parameters relate under inheritance: covariance (out, producers), contravariance (in, consumers), invariance by default, and unsafe array covariance.

title: Reflection & Attributes
description: Inspect and manipulate types at runtime — read metadata and attributes, create instances, invoke methods — powering DI, serializers and ORMs at a performance cost.

title: yield & Iterators
description: How yield return builds a lazy iterator: the compiler-generated state machine producing a sequence one element at a time, enabling deferred execution and streaming.

title: Extension Methods
description: Add methods to existing types you don't own (string, IEnumerable) without modifying or subclassing them — a static method with a this parameter, as LINQ uses.

title: Two Sum
description: Return the indices of the two numbers that add up to a target. Brute-force O(n²) vs the hash-map O(n) one-pass solution and the complement pattern.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Detect whether any value appears twice. Brute-force, sort, and the optimal HashSet O(n) solution — the 'use a set for uniqueness' pattern.

title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's)
description: Find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum using Kadane's algorithm — O(n) time, O(1) space — with the extend-or-restart running-sum insight.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Return the product of all other elements without division in O(n), using the prefix-and-suffix products technique with O(1) extra space.

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Check a string ignoring case and non-alphanumeric characters using the two-pointer technique in O(n) time and O(1) space.

title: 3Sum
description: Find all unique triplets that sum to zero with sort plus two pointers for O(n²), carefully skipping duplicates.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: The sliding-window technique with a last-seen index map for an O(n) solution — the model variable-size window problem.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Maximize profit from one buy and one later sell in a single O(n) pass by tracking the minimum price so far.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Check whether brackets are correctly matched and nested using a stack — the canonical stack problem — in O(n).

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack with push, pop, top and getMin all in O(1) by storing the running minimum alongside each value.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Decide whether two strings are anagrams using a character-frequency count for O(n) time and O(1) space.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Bucket words that are anagrams by hashing each to a canonical key (sorted string or character-count signature).

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Reverse a singly linked list in place with the prev/curr/next three-pointer pattern in O(n) time and O(1) space, plus the recursive variant.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Detect a cycle in a linked list with Floyd's tortoise-and-hare slow/fast pointers in O(n) time and O(1) space — no hash set needed.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Splice two sorted linked lists into one using a dummy head and a tail pointer — the two-pointer merge step of merge sort — in O(n+m) time.

title: Binary Search
description: Find a target in a sorted array by halving the range each step — O(log n) — with the overflow-safe midpoint and a consistent loop invariant.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Find a target in a rotated sorted array in O(log n) by spotting that one half of every split is still sorted and range-checking into it.

title: Kth Largest Element
description: Find the kth largest value with a size-k min-heap in O(n log k), or Quickselect for O(n) average — the canonical top-k pattern.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Mirror a binary tree by swapping every node's children — the textbook recursive tree problem — in O(n), with an iterative queue variant.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Compute a tree's height as 1 + max(left depth, right depth) — the foundational tree recurrence — in O(n), with a BFS level-count alternative.

title: Validate BST
description: Validate a binary search tree by carrying a (min, max) bound down each branch — not just comparing parent and child — in O(n), plus the in-order check.

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Traverse a tree level by level with BFS, freezing the queue size each pass to group nodes by depth, in O(n).

title: Number of Islands
description: Count connected land components in a grid by flood-filling each new island with DFS/BFS and marking cells visited — O(rows×cols).

title: Course Schedule
description: Decide if prerequisites are completable: detect a cycle in a directed graph via Kahn's topological sort (in-degree BFS) in O(V+E).

title: Subsets
description: Generate the power set with the choose/explore/un-choose backtracking template and a start index — O(n·2^n).

title: Combination Sum
description: Find all combinations summing to a target (candidates reusable) via backtracking with a start index and sort-and-break pruning.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: Count ways to climb n stairs taking 1 or 2 steps — the Fibonacci recurrence — with O(n) bottom-up DP reduced to O(1) space.

title: Coin Change
description: Fewest coins to make an amount via bottom-up DP: dp[a] = 1 + min(dp[a-coin]) — why greedy fails — in O(amount×coins).

title: House Robber
description: Maximize loot without robbing adjacent houses: dp[i] = max(skip, rob this + dp[i-2]), reduced to O(1) rolling variables.

title: Single Number
description: Find the element that appears once while others appear twice using XOR (a^a=0 cancels pairs) — O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Pow(x, n)
description: Compute x^n in O(log n) by exponentiation by squaring — square the base, halve the exponent — handling negative powers and int.MinValue.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Maximize water between two lines with an opposite-end two-pointer sweep that always moves the shorter wall inward — O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Compute trapped rain as min(maxLeft, maxRight) − height per column, reduced from prefix arrays to an O(1)-space two-pointer pass.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Return the k most frequent values by counting then bucket-sorting by frequency for O(n), or using a size-k heap for O(n log k).

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description: Find the longest run of consecutive integers in O(n) with a HashSet, counting only from sequence starts (n−1 not present).

title: Merge Intervals
description: Merge overlapping intervals by sorting on start, then sweeping and extending the last interval whenever the next overlaps — O(n log n).

title: Insert Interval
description: Insert a new interval into a sorted non-overlapping list in O(n): emit the before block, fold overlaps into the new interval, then emit the rest.

title: Remove Nth Node From End
description: Remove the nth node from the end in one pass using two pointers spaced n apart, with a dummy head to handle removing the first node.

title: Reorder List
description: Reorder a list to L0→Ln→L1→Ln-1… by composing find-middle, reverse-second-half, and merge-alternately — O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Add Two Numbers
description: Add two numbers stored as reversed-digit linked lists by simulating long addition with a running carry and a dummy head — O(max(n,m)).

title: Same Tree
description: Check if two binary trees are structurally identical with equal values using a parallel recursion comparing both nodes at once — O(n).

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a BST
description: Find the LCA in a BST by walking from the root: go left if both targets are smaller, right if both larger, else this split node is the answer — O(h).

title: Diameter of Binary Tree
description: Find the longest path between any two nodes by computing height bottom-up and tracking leftHeight + rightHeight at each node — O(n).

title: Clone Graph
description: Deep-copy a connected undirected graph with DFS/BFS and an old→new visited map that both dedupes shared nodes and breaks cycles — O(V+E).

title: Rotting Oranges
description: Find the minutes for rot to spread across a grid with multi-source BFS: seed all rotten cells, expand one level per minute, then check for unreachable fresh cells.

title: Jump Game
description: Decide if you can reach the last index when each value is a max jump length, using a greedy farthest-reachable scan — O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description: Longest substring of one repeated letter after at most k replacements: a sliding window valid while window length − most frequent char count ≤ k — O(n).

title: Permutation in String
description: Detect whether one string contains a permutation of another using a fixed-size sliding window over matching character-frequency counts — O(n).

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Find the smallest substring of s containing all characters of t with a grow-then-shrink window and a have/required match counter — O(|s|+|t|).

title: Search a 2D Matrix
description: Search a row-joined sorted matrix by treating it as one sorted array of m×n and binary searching with index/cols and index%cols mapping — O(log mn).

title: Koko Eating Bananas
description: Find the minimum eating speed to finish in h hours by binary searching on the answer, since feasibility is monotonic in speed — O(n log max pile).

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Find the minimum of a rotated sorted array in O(log n) by binary searching for the inflection point, comparing mid to the right end to pick a half.

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate a postfix expression with a stack: push numbers, and on an operator pop two operands, apply, push the result — O(n) time, O(n) space.

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Generate all valid combinations of n pairs of parentheses with backtracking, adding '(' while open < n and ')' while close < open to prune invalid branches.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: For each day, how many days until a warmer one — a monotonic decreasing stack of indices that resolves each day when a warmer temperature arrives — O(n).

title: Permutations
description: Generate all orderings of distinct numbers with backtracking: at each position try every unused element, mark used, recurse, then unmark — O(n·n!).

title: Word Search
description: Decide if a word exists in a grid by DFS backtracking from each cell, marking cells visited during a path and restoring them on return — O(m·n·4^L).

title: Palindrome Partitioning
description: Partition a string so every piece is a palindrome using backtracking over prefix cut points, recursing on the remainder when a prefix is a palindrome — O(n·2^n).

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence: the O(n^2) ending-at-i DP and the O(n log n) patience-sorting tails-with-binary-search trick.

title: Word Break
description: Decide if a string can be segmented into dictionary words with reachability DP: dp[i] true if some dp[j] is true and s[j..i] is in the dictionary — O(n^2).

title: Maximum Product Subarray
description: Largest product of a contiguous subarray: track both running max and min because a negative flips them, updating both each step — O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Subtree of Another Tree
description: Check whether one tree is a subtree of another by trying a same-tree comparison rooted at every node — O(m·n) — reusing the identical-tree recursion, with a C# solution.

title: Symmetric Tree
description: Check whether a binary tree is a mirror of itself by recursing on outer and inner pairs of two subtrees in lockstep — O(n) — with a C# solution and the mirror pattern.

title: Balanced Binary Tree
description: Check whether a binary tree is height-balanced in O(n) by computing height bottom-up and short-circuiting with a sentinel when any subtree differs by more than 1 — C# solution.

title: Binary Tree Right Side View
description: Return the values visible from the right of a binary tree using level-order BFS and taking the last node of each level — O(n) — with a C# solution and the pattern.

title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
description: Count nodes whose value is at least the maximum on the path from the root, using a DFS that carries the running max down each branch — O(n) — with a C# solution.

title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
description: Find the kth smallest value in a BST with an in-order traversal that yields values in sorted order, stopping at the kth — O(h + k) — with an iterative C# solution.

title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder
description: Rebuild a binary tree from preorder and inorder traversals: the preorder head is the root, and its index in inorder splits left and right subtrees — O(n) with a hash map — C#.

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
description: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree where a path bends at one node: return the best downward gain up the recursion while updating a global best with left+node+right — O(n) — C#.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Encode a binary tree to a string and rebuild it using preorder DFS with explicit null markers, so structure is preserved — O(n) each way — with a C# solution.

title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: Build a prefix tree supporting insert, search, and startsWith, where each node holds child links and an end-of-word flag — O(L) per operation — with a C# solution.

title: Design Add and Search Words
description: Build a dictionary supporting addWord and search where "." matches any letter, using a trie with DFS branching on wildcards — with a C# solution and the pattern.

title: Word Search II
description: Find all dictionary words in a grid efficiently by building a trie of the words and DFS-ing the board once, pruning paths that leave the trie — far better than searching each word — C#.

title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
description: Maintain the kth largest value as numbers arrive using a size-k min-heap whose root is always the answer — O(log k) per add — with a C# PriorityQueue solution.

title: Last Stone Weight
description: Repeatedly smash the two heaviest stones until one or none remains, using a max-heap to always fetch the top two in O(log n) — with a C# PriorityQueue solution.

title: K Closest Points to Origin
description: Return the k points nearest the origin using a size-k max-heap by squared distance, evicting the farthest — O(n log k) — or Quickselect for O(n) average — with a C# solution.

title: Task Scheduler
description: Find the minimum time to run tasks with a cooldown n between identical tasks: a math formula from the most frequent task, or a greedy max-heap simulation — with a C# solution.

title: Design Twitter
description: Design a mini Twitter with postTweet, follow, unfollow, and a news feed that merges the 10 most recent tweets from followees using a max-heap by timestamp — with a C# solution.

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Maintain a running median with two heaps — a max-heap for the lower half and a min-heap for the upper half — kept balanced so the median is read off the tops — O(log n) add — C#.

title: Subsets II
description: Generate all unique subsets when the input has duplicates by sorting and skipping a duplicate at the same recursion depth unless it follows its identical predecessor — O(n·2^n) — C#.

title: Combination Sum II
description: Find unique combinations summing to a target where each number is used at most once and the input has duplicates: sort, advance the index, and skip same-level duplicates — C#.

title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
description: Generate all letter strings a phone number could spell by backtracking through each digit’s letters, building one character per recursion level — O(4^n·n) — with a C# solution.

title: N-Queens
description: Place n queens on an n×n board with none attacking by backtracking row by row, tracking used columns and both diagonals as sets for O(1) checks — with a C# solution.

title: Jump Game II
description: Find the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index by greedily treating each jump as a BFS level: extend the current reachable window and bump the jump count at its boundary — O(n) — C#.

title: Gas Station
description: Find the start station to complete a circular route: if total gas ≥ total cost a solution exists, and the unique start is just after wherever the running tank goes negative — O(n) — C#.

title: Hand of Straights
description: Decide if a hand can be rearranged into groups of consecutive cards of a given size by greedily starting each group at the smallest remaining card, tracked with a sorted count map — C#.

title: Partition Labels
description: Cut a string into the most parts so each letter appears in only one part: record each letter’s last index, then extend the current part to the max last-index of its letters — O(n) — C#.

title: Valid Parenthesis String
description: Decide if a string of (, ), and wildcard * can be a valid parenthesization by tracking the range [low, high] of possible open-paren counts in one greedy pass — O(n), O(1) — C#.

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
description: Find the minimum intervals to remove so the rest don’t overlap by greedily sorting on end time and keeping intervals that start at or after the last kept end — O(n log n) — C#.

title: Meeting Rooms
description: Decide if a person can attend all meetings by sorting intervals on start time and checking that no meeting begins before the previous one ends — O(n log n) — with a C# solution.

title: Meeting Rooms II
description: Find the minimum meeting rooms needed by tracking concurrent meetings: a min-heap of end times, or sorted start/end sweep counting overlaps — O(n log n) — with a C# solution.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Count set bits in an integer with the Brian Kernighan trick n &= n-1, which clears the lowest set bit each step so the loop runs once per 1-bit — with a C# solution.

title: Counting Bits
description: Compute the set-bit count for every integer 0..n in O(n) using the DP dp[i] = dp[i >> 1] + (i & 1), reusing the already-computed count of i with its last bit dropped — C#.

title: Reverse Bits
description: Reverse the 32 bits of an unsigned integer by pulling the lowest bit off the input and pushing it onto a result that is shifted left each step — O(32) — with a C# solution.

title: Missing Number
description: Find the missing value in 0..n either by XOR-ing all indices with all values so pairs cancel, or by subtracting the array sum from n(n+1)/2 — O(n) time, O(1) space — C#.

title: Sum of Two Integers
description: Add two integers without + or - using bitwise ops: XOR gives the sum without carry, AND-and-shift gives the carry, repeat until the carry is zero — with a C# solution.

title: Reverse Integer
description: Reverse the digits of a signed 32-bit integer and return 0 on overflow by checking the running result against the int bounds before each digit push — O(log n) — with a C# solution.

title: House Robber II
description: Rob a circular street of houses where the first and last are adjacent by running the linear House Robber twice — once excluding the first house, once excluding the last — and taking the max — C#.

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Find the longest palindromic substring by expanding around each of the 2n-1 centers and keeping the widest palindrome — O(n^2) time, O(1) space — with a C# solution.

title: Palindromic Substrings
description: Count all palindromic substrings by expanding around each of the 2n-1 centers and counting every successful expansion — O(n^2) time, O(1) space — with a C# solution.

title: Decode Ways
description: Count the ways to decode a digit string into letters (A=1..Z=26) with a Fibonacci-like DP: at each position add the ways from a valid one-digit and two-digit step — O(n) — C#.

title: Unique Paths
description: Count paths from the top-left to bottom-right of a grid moving only right or down: a 2-D DP where each cell sums the cell above and the cell to its left, reducible to one row — O(m·n).

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Find the length of the longest subsequence common to two strings with a 2-D DP: matching characters extend the diagonal, mismatches take the best of dropping one character — O(m·n).

title: Edit Distance
description: Compute the minimum insert, delete, or replace operations to turn one string into another with a 2-D DP: equal chars carry the diagonal, else 1 + min of the three neighbors — O(m·n).

title: Coin Change II
description: Count the number of ways to make an amount with unlimited coins using a 1-D DP iterated coin-by-coin so each combination is counted once regardless of order — O(amount·coins).

title: Target Sum
description: Count ways to assign + and - to numbers so they sum to a target by transforming it into a subset-sum count: find subsets summing to (total+target)/2 with a 1-D DP — O(n·sum).

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
description: Maximize stock profit with unlimited transactions and a one-day cooldown after selling using a small state machine DP over hold, sold, and rest states — O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Interleaving String
description: Decide whether a string is an interleaving of two others using a 2-D DP where dp[i][j] is reachable if the current char matches s1 or s2 and the smaller state was reachable — O(m·n).

title: Network Delay Time
description: Find how long a signal takes to reach all nodes from a source: shortest paths in a weighted directed graph via Dijkstra with a min-heap, then take the maximum distance — O(E log V).

title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
description: Find the cheapest route with at most k stops using Bellman-Ford limited to k+1 relaxation rounds on a snapshot of distances, which bounds the number of edges used — O(k·E).

title: Reconstruct Itinerary
description: Reconstruct an itinerary using all tickets in lexical order by finding an Eulerian path with Hierholzer’s algorithm: DFS greedily and append nodes post-order, then reverse — O(E log E).

title: Min Cost to Connect All Points
description: Connect all points at minimum total Manhattan distance: a minimum spanning tree built with Prim’s algorithm and a min-heap, repeatedly adding the cheapest edge to a new point — O(n^2 log n).

title: Swim in Rising Water
description: Find the least time to cross a grid where water rises, minimizing the maximum cell elevation on a path: Dijkstra-like search with a min-heap that expands the lowest peak first — O(n^2 log n).

title: Alien Dictionary
description: Derive the letter order of an alien language from a sorted word list by building ordering constraints from adjacent word pairs and topologically sorting them, detecting contradictions — O(C).

title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
description: Find cells from which water can reach both oceans by reverse DFS/BFS from each ocean’s border (climbing to equal or higher cells) and intersecting the two reachable sets — O(m·n).

title: Surrounded Regions
description: Capture regions of O fully surrounded by X by flooding from border-connected O’s first (marking them safe), then flipping every remaining O to X — O(m·n) — with a C# solution.

title: Course Schedule II
description: Return a valid order to take all courses given prerequisites via topological sort (Kahn’s in-degree BFS): output courses as their in-degree hits zero, or empty if a cycle exists — O(V+E).

title: Redundant Connection
description: Find the extra edge that turns a tree into a graph with one cycle using union-find: the first edge whose endpoints already share a root is the redundant one — near O(n) with path compression.

title: Number of Connected Components
description: Count connected components in an undirected graph with union-find: start with n components and decrement each time an edge joins two different sets — near O(n+e) — with a C# solution.

title: Graph Valid Tree
description: Check whether an undirected graph is a tree: it must have exactly n-1 edges and be fully connected with no cycles, verified with union-find or a single DFS — O(n) — with a C# solution.

title: Word Ladder
description: Find the shortest transformation from start to end changing one letter at a time through a dictionary using BFS over words, generating neighbors by wildcard patterns — O(N·L^2) — C#.

title: Walls and Gates
description: Fill each empty room with the distance to its nearest gate using multi-source BFS seeded from all gates at once, so every room is reached by its shortest path first — O(m·n) — C#.

title: Palindrome Linked List
description: Check whether a singly linked list is a palindrome in O(1) space by finding the middle, reversing the second half, and comparing the two halves node by node — O(n) — with a C# solution.

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: Merge k sorted linked lists into one using a min-heap of the current heads, repeatedly popping the smallest and pushing its successor — O(N log k) — or divide-and-conquer pairwise — C#.

title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
description: Reverse a linked list in consecutive groups of k, leaving a trailing remainder under k untouched, by reversing each full group in place and stitching the segments — O(n) time, O(1) space — C#.

title: Copy List with Random Pointer
description: Deep-copy a linked list whose nodes have an extra random pointer using a hash map from original to clone, or an O(1)-space interleaving trick that weaves clones between originals — O(n) — C#.

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a fixed-capacity cache with O(1) get and put by combining a hash map for lookup with a doubly linked list ordering entries by recency, evicting the least recently used tail — C#.

title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
description: Find two numbers in a sorted array that sum to a target using two pointers from both ends that move inward based on the sum versus the target — O(n) time, O(1) space — with a C# solution.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
description: Remove duplicates in place from a sorted array using a slow write pointer that only advances on a new value, returning the count of unique elements — O(n) time, O(1) space — with a C# solution.

title: 4Sum
description: Find all unique quadruples summing to a target by sorting, fixing two indices with nested loops, and two-pointering the rest while skipping duplicates — O(n^3) — with a C# solution.

title: Car Fleet
description: Count how many fleets of cars arrive at a target by sorting cars by start position descending and counting how many arrival times are not swallowed by a slower car ahead — O(n log n) — C#.

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Find the largest rectangle in a histogram with a monotonic increasing stack of bar indices: when a shorter bar appears, pop and compute areas using the popped height and the width between boundaries — O(n).

title: Time Based Key-Value Store
description: Design a store that returns the value of a key at the largest timestamp ≤ a query time by keeping each key’s entries in timestamp order and binary searching for the floor — O(log n) get — C#.

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
description: Find the median of two sorted arrays in O(log(min(m,n))) by binary searching a partition of the smaller array so the combined left half is correct, checked via the four boundary values — C#.

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Return the maximum of every window of size k using a monotonic decreasing deque of indices, so the front is always the window max and each element is pushed and popped once — O(n) — C#.

title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
description: Decide if an array can split into two equal-sum subsets by reducing to a subset-sum DP targeting half the total: a 1-D boolean knapsack iterated in reverse — O(n·sum) — with a C# solution.

title: Happy Number
description: Detect a happy number — repeatedly replacing it by the sum of the squares of its digits reaches 1 — using Floyd’s cycle detection to spot loops without a set — O(log n) — with a C# solution.

title: Plus One
description: Add one to a number represented as a digit array by propagating the carry from the least significant digit, handling the all-nines case by prepending a leading one — O(n) — with a C# solution.

title: Multiply Strings
description: Multiply two non-negative integers given as strings without big-integer types by simulating grade-school multiplication into a digit array, where digits i and j land at positions i+j and i+j+1 — O(m·n).

title: Majority Element
description: Find the element appearing more than n/2 times in O(n) time and O(1) space with the Boyer-Moore voting algorithm: keep a candidate and a count that cancels out minority elements — C#.
